sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 2025년 매출 750억 4,800만 달러를 기록했으나, 미국 정부 의존도와 공급망 및 경쟁 심화 리스크가 지속되고 있습니다.

keyPoints.text: 미국 정부 계약에 대한 높은 의존도가 사업의 핵심이며, 2025년 전체 매출의 72%가 미국 정부로부터 발생했습니다.
국방부(DoW) 매출 비중이 전체의 63%를 차지하고 있어 정부의 예산 우선순위 변화가 실적에 직접적인 영향을 미칩니다.
해외 고객 대상 매출 비중이 28%를 기록하며 글로벌 시장에서의 정치적, 경제적 리스크에 노출되어 있습니다.

riskChanges.text: 미국 정부의 예산 불확실성 및 국방 지출 감소 가능성이 사업 운영과 재무 상태에 중대한 부정적 영향을 줄 수 있습니다.
스타트업 및 비전통적 방산 기업의 진입으로 경쟁이 심화되고 있으며, 기술적 우위 확보 및 비용 효율성 유지가 필수적입니다.
정부 계약 관련 법규 위반 시 계약 해지, 입찰 자격 정지, 수출 제한 등 심각한 제재를 받을 수 있는 규제 리스크가 존재합니다.

businessChanges.text: 경쟁사들의 기술 개발 및 수직 계열화 전략에 대응하여 엔지니어링 및 서비스 역량을 강화해야 하는 과제에 직면해 있습니다.
팬데믹과 같은 공중보건 위기 발생 시 공급망 차질과 인플레이션 압력으로 인한 생산 지연 리스크가 상존합니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025 회계연도 매출은 750억 4,800만 달러, 영업이익은 77억 3,100만 달러를 기록했습니다.
순이익은 50억 1,700만 달러이며, 총자산 598억 4,000만 달러 중 현금 및 현금성 자산은 41억 2,100만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 매출의 72%가 미국 정부에 집중된 구조에서 정부 예산 삭감이나 정책 변화 시 수익성 방어 능력을 면밀히 모니터링해야 합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: FY2025 operating income = 77억 3,100만 달러 (USD 7.731B; 7,731 million), XBRL range 2025-01-01..2025-12-31.
FY2025 net income = 50억 1,700만 달러 (USD 5.017B; 5,017 million), XBRL range 2025-01-01..2025-12-31.
FY2025 total assets = 598억 4,000만 달러 (USD 59.84B; 59,840 million), XBRL range instant..2025-12-31.
FY2025 cash and cash equivalents = 41억 2,100만 달러 (USD 4.121B; 4,121 million), XBRL range instant..2025-12-31.

Risks Related to our Reliance on Government Contracts, our Industry and the Economy We depend heavily on contracts with the U.S. Government for a substantial portion of our business. Changes in the U.S. Government’s priorities, or delays or reductions in spending could have a material adverse effect on our business. We derived 72% of our total consolidated sales from the U.S. Government in 2025, including 63% from the Department of War (DoW), also known as the Department of Defense under 10 U.S.C. § 111(a). We expect to continue to derive most of our sales from work performed under U.S. Government contracts. A reduction in overall U.S. defense spending, on an absolute or inflation-adjusted basis, because of shifting priorities, budget compromises or otherwise could adversely affect our business. Budget uncertainty, extended or repeated U.S. Government shutdowns, the use of continuing res

Our business and reputation could be adversely affected if we or those we do business with fail to comply with these laws, regulations, or terms. We must comply with extensive laws and regulations relating to the award, administration and performance of U.S. Government contracts. Government contract laws and regulations affect how we do business with our customers and impose certain risks and costs on our business. A violation of these laws and regulations by us, our employees, others working on our behalf, a supplier or a joint venture partner could harm our reputation and result in the imposition of fines and penalties, the termination of our contracts, suspension or debarment from bidding on or being awarded contracts, loss of our ability to export products or perform services and civil or criminal investigations or proceedings. From time to time, the U.S. Government has proposed cont

Competition and changing customer procurement policies could adversely affect our business and financial results. We operate in a highly competitive industry and our competitors may have more extensive or more specialized engineering, technical, marketing and servicing capabilities than we do in certain areas. Our competitors may develop new technologies, products or services that could replace our current offerings. Additionally, if competitors can offer lower cost services and products, or provide services or products more quickly, at equivalent or in some cases even reduced capabilities, we may lose new business opportunities or contract recompetes, which could adversely affect our future results. We are facing increased competition from new entrants, startups and non-traditional defense contractors. Furthermore, acquisitions in our industry, including vertical integration, also could

We face a wide variety of risks related to public health crises, epidemics, pandemics or similar events. If a new health epidemic or outbreak were to occur, we could experience broad and varied effects similar to the impact of COVID-19, including adverse impacts to our workforce and supply chain, inflationary pressures and increased costs, schedule or production delays, market volatility and other financial ramifications. If any of these were to occur, our future results and performance could be adversely impacted. International sales may pose different economic, regulatory, competition and other risks. International sales present risks that are different and potentially greater than those encountered in our U.S. business. In 2025, 28% of our total sales were associated with international customers.
